messages.text: The cherry blossoms drifted down like pink snow, catching the golden light of the setting sun.

messages.name: Mei
messages.text: Kazuhiko... I need to tell you something.
Her voice was barely a whisper, trembling with an emotion I could not quite name. The solitary figure beneath the ancient tree looked impossibly small, impossibly fragile.

messages.name: Mei
messages.text: Do you ever feel like some moments are just... ephemeral? Like no matter how hard you try to hold onto them, they slip through your fingers?
Her words resonated somewhere deep inside me. I knew exactly what she meant, but the memory of that summer day felt too distant, too ambiguous to put into words.
